key_insight: SoftVTBench shows that deformable-object manipulation must be judged not only by task completion, but also by whether the robot avoids drop and excessive deformation during execution.
engineering_relevance: This benchmark is useful for designing and comparing physically safe manipulation policies for soft robotics, especially when tactile feedback and deformation control matter.
method: Built in Isaac Sim with FEM-simulated deformable objects and multi-view RGB, RGB tactile marker motion, proprioception, and language inputs.
Defines four matched task suites across deformable vs. rigid objects and object-centric vs. spatial variation.
Reports both Goal Success and Safety Success, where safety also requires no drop and peak deformation below an object-specific threshold from privileged FEM states.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that success-only evaluation overstates performance, and that adding tactile sensing improved Safety Success from 21.4% to 35.6% on object-centric deformable tasks while keeping Goal Success comparable.
watch_item: Safety labels depend on privileged FEM states and calibrated deformation thresholds, so benchmark results may be sensitive to simulator fidelity and threshold selection.
paper_title: SoftVTBench: A Safety-Aware Visuo-Tactile Benchmark for Physically Constrained Robotic Manipulation of Deformable Objects
